confirmed.title: resources.md: Resource::indexQuery() documented with a $query Builder parameter it does not accept
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change to `public function indexQuery(): Builder` and build the query from scratch, e.g. `return Post::query()->where('user_id', auth()->id())->latest();`. Apply to EN/es/pt-BR resources.md line 106 and to reference/php/core.md:21.
confirmed.detail: The guide shows `public function indexQuery(Builder $query): Builder { return $query->where('user_id', auth()->id())->latest(); }`. The real method in packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php:167 is `public function indexQuery(): mixed` — it takes NO parameter and returns a builder/null. It is called with zero args at packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:415 (`$resource->indexQuery()`). The documented body references a `$query` variable that does not exist, so the snippet would not compile. Same wrong signature also lives in the EN reference apps/docs/reference/php/core.md:21 (`indexQuery(Builder) | Builder`) and the es/pt-BR mirrors of this guide (line 106 in each).
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. apps/docs/guide/resources.md:106 documents `public function indexQuery(Builder $query): Builder { return $query->where('user_id', auth()->id())->latest(); }`. The real method at packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php:167 is `public function indexQuery(): mixed` — it takes NO parameter and returns null by default (override returns a builder/null). It is invoked with zero arguments at packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:415 (`$custom = $resource->indexQuery();`). The documented signature is objectively wrong: the method accepts no parameter, so the snippet referencing `$query` would not work as written. The doc is in-scope (apps/docs guide, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated) and not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json. This is a genuine factual-drift contradiction, not a style nit or missing-doc.

confirmed.title: resources.md: beforeSave() shown returning void; actual contract returns array<string,mixed>
confirmed.suggested_fix: Declare `protected function beforeSave(Model $record, array $data): array`, mutate `$data` (or `$record`) and `return $data;` at the end of the example.
confirmed.detail: The code example declares `protected function beforeSave(Model $record, array $data): void` and returns nothing. The real hook in packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php:288 is `protected function beforeSave(Model $record, array $data): array { return $data; }` (mutate-and-return contract). Overriding with `: void` violates the parent return type (fatal error) and drops the mutated data. Present in all three locales (EN/es/pt-BR line 66). Note: reference/php/core.md was already corrected for this family, but resources.md was not.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed on disk. Doc apps/docs/guide/resources.md:66 (and pt-BR/es mirrors at line 66) declares `protected function beforeSave(Model $record, array $data): void` with an example body that mutates $record but returns nothing. The real contract in packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php:288 is `protected function beforeSave(Model $record, array $data): array { return $data; }` with a `@return array<string, mixed>` docblock — a mutate-and-return contract. Overriding with `: void` violates the parent return type (PHP fatal error) and would drop the mutated $data. The doc is in-scope (guide, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). The ledger only fixed this family for apps/docs/reference/php/core.md:27, not for guide/resources.md, so this is not a duplicate. Both sides verified.

confirmed.title: resources.md: presents Resource::actions() as an overridable method; no such method exists
confirmed.suggested_fix: Remove the `actions()` method block and the claim that `actions()` is an optional Resource method; show action declaration only inside `table()` via `->actions([Actions::edit(), Actions::delete()])`. Apply to EN/es/pt-BR.
confirmed.detail: The 'Table and Actions' section states "The `table()` and `actions()` methods are optional" and shows `public function actions(): array { return [...]; }` (lines 116, 136). Resource (packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php) has no `actions()` method — its public methods are effectiveFields, fields, form, indexQuery, recordTitle, recordSubtitle, runCreate, runUpdate, runDelete, table. Row/bulk actions are declared inside `table()` via `->actions([...])`. The ledger already removed `Resource::actions()` from reference/php/core.md, but this guide (and its es/pt-BR mirrors, line 116/136) still document it.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. Doc apps/docs/guide/resources.md:116 states "The `table()` and `actions()` methods are optional — Arqel falls back to automatic derivation when absent" and shows an overridable `public function actions(): array` at line 136. Source packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php has NO actions() method — its public instance methods are fields, indexQuery, table, form, effectiveFields, recordTitle, recordSubtitle, runCreate, runUpdate, runDelete. The HasActions contract (packages/core/src/Contracts/HasActions.php) is an empty marker whose docblock explicitly says actions()/tableActions() "will be added once arqel-dev/actions and arqel-dev/table ship" — i.e. not implemented today. Row/bulk actions are declared via ->actions([...]) inside table() (as the same doc snippet shows at line 133). So presenting a standalone overridable Resource::actions() method is objectively false/misleading. In-scope: it is a user guide under apps/docs/guide, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated. Not in ledger: the existing ledger entry (line 23) targets reference/php/core.md, a different file; this guide and its es/pt-BR mirrors were not covered.

confirmed.title: tables-forms.md: Tab::make()->id()->label() chained API does not exist
confirmed.suggested_fix: Use `Tab::make('content', 'Content')->schema([...])` (id and label as positional constructor args), dropping the nonexistent `->id()` / `->label()` calls. Apply to EN/es/pt-BR tables-forms.md lines 119-121.
confirmed.detail: The Tabs example shows `Tab::make()->id('content')->label('Content')->schema([...])`. The real factory in packages/form/src/Layout/Tab.php:32 is `public static function make(string $id, string $label): self` (two required positional args), and Tab/Component expose no `id()` or `label()` fluent setters (only `icon()`, `badge()`, and inherited `schema()`). `Tab::make()` with no args is a TypeError. Present identically in all three locales (lines 119-121).
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides on disk. Doc apps/docs/guide/tables-forms.md:118-124 shows `Tab::make()->id('content')->label('Content')->schema([...])`. Real source packages/form/src/Layout/Tab.php:32 is `public static function make(string $id, string $label): self` with two required positional args, and the constructor (line 26) requires both. The class exposes only `icon()` (line 37) and `badge()` (line 44) fluent setters plus `getId()`/`getLabel()` getters — there are NO `id()` or `label()` setter methods, and Component (parent) provides no such methods either. Thus `Tab::make()` with no args is a TypeError, and `->id()`/`->label()` calls reference non-existent methods. The doc is objectively false/misleading vs current code. In-scope (apps/docs/guide, not PLANNING/tickets/superpowers/CHANGELOG/generated). Not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (no tables-forms or Tab entries). Present identically in EN, pt-BR, and es locales (lines 119-121 each).

confirmed.title: agents.md: MCP section claims the server is a 'stub in Phase 1, full in Phase 2' and imports a nonexistent ArqelMcpServer from @arqel-dev/mcp
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace the Phase-1/Phase-2 framing and the fictional `ArqelMcpServer`/`@arqel-dev/mcp` snippet with the real shipped state: link to the dedicated MCP guide (/guide/mcp-server), reference `@arqel-dev/mcp-server` (npx -y @arqel-dev/mcp-server) and the 7 real tools (search_docs, get_adr, get_api_reference, list_resources, describe_resource, generate_resource, generate_field). Apply to EN/es/pt-BR.
confirmed.detail: Lines 112-130 frame the MCP server as "stub in Phase 1, full in Phase 2" with a 'Phase 2 preview' code block `import { ArqelMcpServer } from '@arqel-dev/mcp'; const server = new ArqelMcpServer({ panel: 'admin' })`. Reality: (1) the MCP server is SHIPPED — packages/mcp (composer `arqel-dev/mcp`) implements `McpServer` JSON-RPC + tool/resource/prompt registries, and packages-js/mcp-server (npm `@arqel-dev/mcp-server`) ships 7 tools; the package's own README.md already says 'Shipped'. (2) `ArqelMcpServer` does not exist anywhere in the codebase. (3) `@arqel-dev/mcp` is not a published npm name — the npm package is `@arqel-dev/mcp-server`. Same stale block in es (line 112/116) and pt-BR (line 112/116) mirrors.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides on disk. DOC (apps/docs/guide/agents.md:110-130) states the MCP server is a "stub in Phase 1, full in Phase 2" and shows a "Phase 2 preview" block: `import { ArqelMcpServer } from '@arqel-dev/mcp'; const server = new ArqelMcpServer({ panel: 'admin' })`. REALITY contradicts all three points: (1) The MCP server is shipped — packages/mcp (composer arqel-dev/mcp) implements McpServer.php with Tools/Resources/Prompts registries, and packages-js/mcp-server (npm @arqel-dev/mcp-server) ships 7 real tools (list-resources, describe-resource, generate-field, generate-resource, get-adr, get-api-reference, search-docs). (2) grep for ArqelMcpServer across ts/tsx/js/php returns zero files — the symbol does not exist. (3) The npm package is @arqel-dev/mcp-server, not @arqel-dev/mcp (that string is the composer/PHP name). The file is in-scope product docs (not PLANNING/tickets/superpowers/CHANGELOG/generated). Ledger has no entry for agents.md or ArqelMcpServer; its mcp-related entries are for advanced/mcp.md (link depth) and packages/mcp/README.md (registry status) — different files/issues. Not a wording nit: the import and class name are objectively false and the phase-status is stale vs shipped code.

confirmed.title: realtime-collab.md: claims arqel-dev/realtime ships with the arqel-dev/framework meta-package
confirmed.suggested_fix: State that `arqel-dev/realtime` is a separate install (`composer require arqel-dev/realtime`) not bundled by the meta-package. Apply to EN/es/pt-BR line 20.
confirmed.detail: Prerequisites state "`arqel-dev/realtime` installed and booted (already shipped in any project that installed the `arqel-dev/framework` meta-package)". The meta-package require list in packages/arqel/composer.json is core, auth, fields, form, actions, nav, table, widgets + inertiajs/inertia-laravel — it does NOT include realtime, and no member package requires it transitively. Users must `composer require arqel-dev/realtime` separately. Same false claim in es (line 20: 'ya incluido en cualquier proyecto...') and pt-BR (line 20: 'já vem em qualquer projeto...').
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides on disk. apps/docs/guide/realtime-collab.md:20 (and pt-BR:20, es:20) state that `arqel-dev/realtime` is "already shipped in any project that installed the `arqel-dev/framework` meta-package". The actual meta-package at packages/arqel/composer.json (type metapackage) requires only: arqel-dev/core, auth, fields, form, actions, nav, table, widgets + inertiajs/inertia-laravel. It does NOT include arqel-dev/realtime. Grepping all packages/*/composer.json for "arqel-dev/realtime" returns only packages/realtime/composer.json (the package referencing itself), so no member package pulls it in transitively. Thus the prerequisite claim is objectively false in all three locales; users must `composer require arqel-dev/realtime` separately (the doc's own Installation section even instructs reverb install/migrate steps). In-scope (apps/docs/guide, not PLANNING/generated/CHANGELOG). Not in ledger — the ledger's realtime entries cover packages/realtime/SKILL.md hooks/components, not this guide's meta-package claim.

confirmed.title: panels.md: tenant() fluent method labeled '(Phase 2)' though the method and the tenant package are shipped
confirmed.suggested_fix: Drop the '(Phase 2)' tag and correct the signature to `tenant(?string $scope = null)` described as setting the panel's tenant scope. Apply to EN/es/pt-BR line 47.
confirmed.detail: The fluent-API table row reads `| tenant(class-string) | class-string | Tenant resolver (Phase 2) |`. The method exists today (packages/core/src/Panel/Panel.php:262 `public function tenant(?string $scope = null): self`) and the tenant package (`arqel-dev/tenant`) is shipped — its README/SKILL were already de-scaffolded in earlier rounds. The '(Phase 2)' label is stale. Additionally the signature is mischaracterized: it takes a `?string $scope`, not a `class-string`, and it sets the panel's tenant scope, not a 'resolver' (the resolver is configured via `config('arqel.tenancy.resolver')`). Same in es (line 47, 'Fase 2') and pt-BR (line 47, 'Phase 2').
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. Doc apps/docs/guide/panels.md:47 reads `| tenant(class-string) | class-string | Tenant resolver (Phase 2) |`. Reality at packages/core/src/Panel/Panel.php:262 is `public function tenant(?string $scope = null): self` which sets `$this->tenantScope` — so (a) the parameter is `?string $scope`, NOT `class-string`; (b) it sets the panel's tenant scope, NOT a resolver. The resolver is configured separately via `arqel.tenancy.resolver` (TenantServiceProvider.php:92, `$config->get('arqel.tenancy.resolver')`). The `(Phase 2)` label is stale: the arqel-dev/tenant package is shipped (TenantManager, working resolvers and feature tests on disk) and its README was already de-scaffolded in an earlier round (ledger line 521). Same drift present in es (line 47, 'Fase 2') and pt-BR (line 47, 'Phase 2'). Doc is in-scope (apps/docs/guide/, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). Ledger has no entry for apps/docs/guide/panels.md — the only tenant-related ledger entry is packages/tenant/README.md, a different file. Three independent factual contradictions confirmed.

confirmed.title: Docs reference a nonexistent `arqel:install --update` flag
confirmed.suggested_fix: Drop `--update` — the sentence already lists `composer update` as the real update path. Either remove the `arqel:install --update` clause entirely or replace it with `composer update`. Fix all three mirrors (EN/PT-BR/ES line 226).
confirmed.detail: Prose tells users that plugin updates are done 'via `arqel:install --update`'. The actual InstallCommand signature in packages/core/src/Commands/InstallCommand.php only defines `--force` and `--no-frontend` — there is no `--update` option, and no other arqel:* command defines `--update` (verified: grep over packages/*/src signatures). Running `php artisan arqel:install --update` would error. Same false flag appears verbatim in the PT-BR and ES mirrors (apps/docs/pt-BR/marketplace/security-best-practices.md:226 and apps/docs/es/marketplace/security-best-practices.md:226).
confirmed.why_real: CONFIRMED. apps/docs/marketplace/security-best-practices.md:226 states plugin updates are the user's responsibility "via `arqel:install --update` or `composer update`". The real InstallCommand at packages/core/src/Commands/InstallCommand.php (line 22) defines signature `arqel:install` with only `{--force}` and `{--no-frontend}` options — there is no `--update` option. A grep for `--update` across all packages/*/src/Commands/*.php returned zero matches, and reviewing every arqel:* command signature (arqel:install, arqel:resource, arqel:make-user, arqel:field, arqel:widget, arqel:dashboard, arqel:tenant:*) confirms none define `--update`. Running `php artisan arqel:install --update` would fail with an unknown-option error. The same false flag appears verbatim in the PT-BR mirror (apps/docs/pt-BR/.../security-best-practices.md:226) and ES mirror (apps/docs/es/.../security-best-practices.md:226). The doc is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). Not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (its 5 'install' entries concern apps/demo scaffolding and meta-package lists in installation.md/README, not this --update flag). The claim is objectively false and misleading vs. current code.

Fix all three mirrors (lines 120 and the usage at 138).
confirmed.detail: The `<VersionTimeline>` React example imports `import { useArqelEndpoint } from '@arqel-dev/core/hooks';` (line 120) and then uses `const { data, isLoading } = useArqelEndpoint<...>(...)` (line 138). Neither exists: (a) `useArqelEndpoint` is defined nowhere in the repo (grep over packages-js/**/*.ts{,x} returns zero non-doc hits); (b) there is no npm package `@arqel-dev/core` — the published JS scopes are listed in packages-js/*/package.json (hooks live in `@arqel-dev/hooks`, whose exports are useResource/useAction/useArqelForm/useBreakpoint/useCanAccess/useFieldDependencies/useFlash/useNavigation/useResourceUpdates/useTable — none named useArqelEndpoint). The snippet will not compile against the real packages. Same broken import is mirrored in apps/docs/pt-BR/examples/versioning/cms-articles.md:120 and apps/docs/es/examples/versioning/cms-articles.md:120.
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. apps/docs/examples/versioning/cms-articles.md:120 imports `useArqelEndpoint` from `@arqel-dev/core/hooks` and uses it at line 138 (`const { data, isLoading } = useArqelEndpoint<...>('arqel.versioning.history', ...)`). Contradicting reality: (a) `useArqelEndpoint` appears in zero source files — grep over packages-js/*/src/ returns no hits; (b) there is no `@arqel-dev/core` JS package (no packages-js/core dir, no package.json declaring that name); (c) hooks actually live in `@arqel-dev/hooks`, whose index.ts exports useAction, useArqelForm, useBreakpoint, useCanAccess, useFieldDependencies, useFlash, useNavigation, useArqelOptimistic, useResource, useResourceUpdates, useTable — none named useArqelEndpoint. So the import path and the hook are both nonexistent and the snippet cannot compile against real packages. File is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/superpowers/CHANGELOG/generated) and not in the ledger (grep of reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json for useArqelEndpoint/cms-articles returned nothing). The mcp-server hits are bundled doc copies, not source, so they do not refute the claim.

confirmed.title: cost-estimation.md claims `@arqel-dev/table` caches `getRecords()` with `cache()->remember()` — no such caching or method exists
confirmed.suggested_fix: Remove or rewrite tip #1: the PHP table package (`arqel-dev/table`) does not cache `getRecords()` by default (the method and the caching layer don't exist). If query-result caching is the intended guidance, present it as a user-side pattern (wrap your Eloquent query in `Cache::remember(...)`) rather than a built-in default, and use the unscoped composer name `arqel-dev/table`. Fix all three mirrors (line 96).
confirmed.detail: Optimization tip #1 states: '`@arqel-dev/table` uses `cache()->remember()` by default for `getRecords()` queries.' Two problems against packages/table/src: (1) the table package contains NO caching at all — grep for `Cache`/`cache(`/`->remember(` over packages/table/src returns zero matches; (2) there is no `getRecords()` method on any table class (grep returns none). The advice to 'bump the TTL to 60s' describes behavior that does not exist. Additionally the package is mislabeled with an npm scope `@arqel-dev/table`; table is the PHP composer package `arqel-dev/table` (no `@`, no npm publish — packages-js/ has no table package). Same false claim in PT-BR and ES mirrors (apps/docs/pt-BR/laravel-cloud/cost-estimation.md:96, apps/docs/es/laravel-cloud/cost-estimation.md:96).
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides on disk. apps/docs/laravel-cloud/cost-estimation.md:96-98 states '@arqel-dev/table uses cache()->remember() by default for getRecords() queries. Bump the TTL to 60s'. Reality in packages/table/src: (1) grep -rniE 'cache|->remember\(|getRecords' over the entire src tree returns ZERO matches — no caching layer exists anywhere in the table package; (2) there is no getRecords() method — the query method is paginate() in TableQueryBuilder.php:130. The 'bump the TTL to 60s' advice describes behavior that does not exist. Also the scope is wrong: composer.json name is 'arqel-dev/table' (PHP composer, no @ npm scope) and packages-js/ has no table package, so '@arqel-dev/table' is mislabeled. Doc is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated), checkable, and not in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json. The claim genuinely contradicts current code.
